title: Best Cordless Grass Shears of 2026: Match Power to Your Trimming Needs
excerpt: Which cordless grass shears survive real yard work? We help gardeners match voltage and build to their trimming tasks, avoiding fragile plastic gears.
author: Editorial Team
metaDescription: Which cordless grass shears survive real yard work? We help gardeners match voltage and build to their trimming tasks, avoiding fragile plastic gears.
intro:
A cordless grass shear that can trim both lawn edges and shrubbery sounds perfect—until the plastic gears strip on the first woody stem. Many budget shears come with blades that look capable, but inside, lightweight drive components fail under moderate stress.

The real measure of a grass shear isn't its sticker features, but what it was engineered to cut. Our guide breaks down the hidden trade-offs—voltage, blade material, and gear design—so you can match a tool to your yard's demands. The picks below reflect which models consistently hold up for their intended trimming tasks, from delicate grass edging to thick ground cover.

Whether you need a featherweight edger for flower beds or a 20V workhorse for ornamental grasses, the right choice saves you from a tool that gives up mid-season.

products.title: WORKPRO Cordless Grass Shear & Shrubbery Trimmer - 2 in 1 Handheld 7.2V Electric Grass Trimmer Hedge Shears/Grass Cutter Rechargeable Lithium-Ion Battery and Type-C Cable Included
products.label: WORKPRO 7.2V Shear & Trimmer
products.oneLiner: Ultra-light 0.82 lb shear with USB-C charging, perfect for delicate grass edging.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Stick with the included USB-C cable for charging; some third-party cables may prevent a proper charge cycle.
pros: Extremely light and maneuverable, ideal for one-handed operation and users with reduced hand strength — 550 mentions, 97% positive — praised for lightness and maneuverability
Tool-free blade swap and intuitive safety button make it beginner-friendly out of the box — 780 mentions, 92% positive — intuitive safety button location and tool-free blade swap
Genuinely versatile 2-in-1 design tackles grass edging and light shrub shaping with the same tool — 321 mentions, 98% positive — capable across lawn edges, flower beds, and small bushes
Clean cutting action on soft grass and thin stems produces a neatly trimmed look — 504 mentions, 84% positive — clean cuts on grass and thin branches
cons: Charging may be finicky with non-standard USB-C cables, and battery longevity could degrade under constant heavy use — 646 mentions, 20% negative — occasional reports of failure to charge or short runtime
products.verdict: For quick grass edging and shrub polish, this is the lightest shear you can buy, and the 2-in-1 swap makes it an outstanding value. Just plan on short, delicate sessions — not an afternoon of continuous hedge work.
products.review:
At just 0.82 pounds, the WORKPRO shear is the lightest usable tool in this class, floating through grass edges and dainty bush trims with barely a whisper of effort. The narrow grip and intuitive safety-button placement let you maneuver one-handed around flower beds, stone paths, and tight corners where heavier shears would strain your wrist. For gardeners with reduced hand strength or anyone who dreads lugging a full-size trimmer for five-minute touch-ups, the sheer ease of handling is the primary reason this tool earns a spot in the shed.

The 2-in-1 design swaps between a 4.17-inch grass blade and a 5.9-inch shrub blade without tools, letting you tidy lawn edges then immediately shape a boxwood with the same unit. The 1100 RPM motor makes clean, scissor-like cuts on soft grass and thin herbaceous stems, though it will bog down if you push it into anything thicker than a pencil. This isn’t a hedge trimmer replacement — it’s a precision detailer that handles the fussy spots your string trimmer can’t reach.

USB-C charging brings modern convenience to a budget shear, eliminating proprietary chargers from your drawer. In practice, you’ll want to use the included cable; some third-party USB-C cords may fail to start a charge cycle, and the battery may not hold a full charge if you run it hard daily for an entire growing season. For the typical user who pulls it out for a few minutes once a week, the 2000mAh pack provides enough runtime to tidy edges, spot-trim a shrub, and put it back without hunting for the charger.

This shear is built for the gardener who wants a guilt-free, responsive little helper for quick grass edging and shrub polishing — not a machine to lean on through a whole-yard renovation. It slots naturally into the hands of first-time cordless buyers, elderly users, or anyone maintaining a compact garden. If your yard demands all-day hedge work or you need to cut branches thicker than a quarter-inch, this tool will disappoint; step up to a 20V model with more backbone. Accept those boundaries, and the WORKPRO’s weightless one-handed operation and dead-simple blade swaps make it a stand-out value in the lightweight category.

💡 💡 Tip: Stick with the included USB-C cable for charging; some third-party cables may prevent a proper charge cycle.

products.title: WORX 2 in 1 Cordless Hedge Trimmer, 4" Grass Shear & 8" Shrub Trimmer with 2 Blades, Battery & Charger Included, WG801
products.label: WORX WG801 20V PowerShare 2-in-1 Grass Shear & Shrub Trimmer
products.oneLiner: 20V interchangeable battery fits 75+ WORX tools for effortless hedges and grass.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: After locking a blade, pull on it gently to verify the latch is engaged. The click alone may not guarantee a secure fit.
pros: Cleanly edges grass and trims light shrubbery without bogging down — 305 mentions, 88% positive — effectively edges lawns and trims small bushes
At 2 pounds, reduces arm fatigue and remains easy to control — 149 mentions, 95% positive — well balanced for extended use
Runtime covers typical yard tasks, and battery fits 75+ other WORX tools — 156 mentions, 88% positive — runtime suits typical yard tasks, and battery swaps across other WORX tools
Blade changes snap in quickly, with a grip that stays comfortable — 185 mentions, 96% positive — seniors and casual gardeners find it effortless
cons: Blades can pop off if the latch isn’t fully seated — requires a pre-use tug test — 81 mentions, 40% negative — blades may pop off during operation or the unit fails after limited use
In a minority of cases, the motor or gear may stop working after only a few uses — 81 mentions, 40% negative — unit fails after limited use
products.verdict: If you already have WORX PowerShare tools, the WG801 is a low-friction addition that handles most grass and shrub touch-ups without swapping battery chargers. Existing PowerShare owners get the most value; those starting fresh should weigh the peace of a 3-year warranty over ecosystem convenience.
products.review:
The WG801 is a natural extension for any WORX PowerShare kit, running on the same 20V batteries that power your drill and string trimmer. Unlike the warranty-backed alternative that requires a separate battery line, this trimmer keeps your shed unified — a meaningful advantage for casual maintenance.

Grass edging cuts cleanly along lawn borders and garden paths, with the 4-inch blade handling fescue and thin weeds without hesitation. Switching to the 8-inch shrub blade, the 1100 RPM motor shapes tender boxwood and new-growth hedges tidily, though quarter-inch woody stems will stop it promptly. A standard 2.0Ah battery runs long enough to edge a typical suburban front yard and trim a few ornamental bushes, matching expectations for a tool this size.

Seniors and casual gardeners appreciate the 2-pound weight and balanced grip that keep arms fresh during 30-minute tidy-ups. The tradeoff is the blade latch: if not fully seated with an audible click, the active blade may detach mid-stroke. The behavior is a known quirk — not a constant failure — and a quick tug test before each use prevents surprises. That said, if you’d trade ecosystem simplicity for a longer warranty and more conservative build, the similarly priced option with a 3-year warranty warrants a look.

💡 💡 Tip: After locking a blade, pull on it gently to verify the latch is engaged. The click alone may not guarantee a secure fit.

products.label: CRAFTSMAN V20 Shear/Trimmer

products.oneLiner: 3-year warranty backed by a V20 battery system, easy grip for seniors.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: A spare V20 battery makes all the difference for larger hedges or thick ground cover — the included 1.5Ah cell is best for quick touch-ups.

Lightweight, low-vibration operation suits seniors and casual gardeners. — 130 mentions, 98% positive — lightweight and intuitive operation

products.review:
The standout here is the 3-year limited warranty, which eclipses the typical 1- to 2-year offerings in the compact shear category, and the construction feels more substantial than the WORX PowerShare alternative.

With the included 1.5Ah battery, a typical morning of edging around flower beds and light shrub shaping is within reach. The 8-inch hedge blade handles small-diameter greenery without drama, and swapping to the 4-inch grass shear is a quick, tool-free process. Pushing through dense, woody growth will drain the battery faster, but for upkeep rather than overhaul, runtime aligns with expectations.

This trimmer makes the most sense for two groups: existing Craftsman V20 users who can share batteries across tools, and seniors or arthritis-prone gardeners who appreciate the low-vibration, lightweight grip. If your trimming routine spans larger hedges or multiple thick patches, you'll want a second battery — the 1.5Ah cell simply isn't built for marathon sessions.

💡 💡 Tip: A spare V20 battery makes all the difference for larger hedges or thick ground cover — the included 1.5Ah cell is best for quick touch-ups.

products.oneLiner: Budget-friendly 7.2V trimmer for delicate grass edging, weighing under 2.5 lbs.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Keep blades free of sap and debris; increased resistance puts extra stress on the plastic drivetrain.
pros: Very low cost for a 2-in-1 shear that handles daily grass touch-ups. — 1099 mentions, 75% positive — works well for daily touch-ups

2-in-1 design swaps easily between grass shear and hedge trimmer blades.
cons: Plastic gear teeth can strip when attempting thicker stems or woody material. — 332 mentions, 68% negative — gears strip or teeth break when cutting anything thicker than grass
products.verdict: If you need a no-regrets disposable shear for one summer of light edging, the low price is hard to beat—but anyone who regularly cuts anything beyond soft grass should look at 20V options.
products.review:
With one of the largest user bases among budget shears, this Sun Joe model has accumulated enough feedback to reveal exactly where it thrives—soft grass trimming and deadheading. Compared to the top pick's ultralight 0.82-pound design and USB-C charging, the HJ604C is heavier but delivers the same interchangeable blades at an easier entry price.

A 7.2V lithium-ion battery provides enough juice for walkway edging and flower bed touch-ups, recharging quickly for the next short session. The tool's 2.5-pound weight feels balanced in hand, making it manageable for seniors or anyone avoiding arm fatigue. The hedge blade clips light new growth on small boxwoods, though it can't handle anything lignified.

Casual gardeners who accept that this is a seasonal tool get the best out of it. Soft grass, clover, and dandelion stems are its limit; any woody material will shear plastic gear teeth. Treat it as a consumable—use it gently, store it dry, and plan to replace it within a year or two if you trim often. That trade-off makes sense for flower bed borders and around garden statues, not for hedging or thick ground cover.

💡 💡 Tip: Keep blades free of sap and debris; increased resistance puts extra stress on the plastic drivetrain.

products.title: Greenworks 24V Cordless Shear Shrubber, 1.5Ah USB Battery and Charger Included
products.label: Greenworks 24V Shear/Shrubber
products.oneLiner: 24V power and 2500 SPM dual-action blades cut through dense ornamental grasses.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: The battery's USB output is handy, but on longer jobs, consider carrying a small power bank if you need to charge multiple devices.
pros: Powerful 24V motor maintains speed through thick ornamental grass and ground cover — 164 mentions, 90% positive — cuts through thick greenery effortlessly
Light 3.5 lb weight and comfortable grip reduce fatigue during longer trimming sessions — 87 mentions, 92% positive — well-received by female users and seniors
Battery's USB port lets you charge a phone or earbuds while you work in the yard — Battery feature praised in multiple reviews for convenience
cons: Motor reliability can vary; some units may stop working after only a few uses — 42 mentions, 67% negative — reliability complaints about motor dying after a few uses
products.verdict: Offers more cutting power and a larger grass blade than many rivals, plus clever USB capability, but longevity remains a roll of the dice.
products.review:
Greenworks' 24V motor and 2500 strokes per minute slice through dense ornamental grasses and tangled ground cover with less bogging down than typical compact shears. The 6-inch grass blade covers more ground per pass, and the 8.5-inch shrub blade swaps in quickly for light hedge shaping. Where many cordless shears struggle once you leave fine lawn grass, this tool keeps its speed, making it a standout for thicker greenery.

At 3.5 pounds, the shear feels substantial but balanced—most users can trim for 20 minutes without arm fatigue. A hidden bright spot is the 1.5Ah battery, which includes a USB-out port to top up a phone right in the garden. No other shear on this list offers that trick, and it turns the battery into a small outdoor power bank during longer sessions.

This tool fits homeowners who frequently cut ornamental grasses, pachysandra, or creeping vines that choke lesser models. Existing Greenworks 24V owners can share batteries, stretching value. The trade-off is longevity: some units have stopped working after only a few uses, so this is a choice for those who prioritize immediate cutting power and the USB perk over long-term certainty. Users with very small hands may also find the 3.5-pound weight less comfortable during one-handed detail work.

💡 💡 Tip: The battery's USB output is handy, but on longer jobs, consider carrying a small power bank if you need to charge multiple devices.

products.title: Makita XMU04Z 18V LXT Lithium-Ion Cordless Grass Shear, Tool Only
products.label: Makita XMU04Z Grass Shear
products.oneLiner: Widest 6-5/16" blade and dual-shear action for hours of trimming on Makita batteries.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Keep the blade guard in place and store the shear flat to prevent bending, and pair it with a 5.0Ah battery for the claimed runtime.
pros: 6-5/16-inch blade width trims more grass per pass, speeding up large bed maintenance. — 101 mentions, 88% positive
Double-shear blade mechanism delivers clean cuts on fescues and boxwoods without tearing. — 82 mentions, 95% positive
Runs up to 200 minutes on a 5.0Ah battery, covering a full morning of trimming. — 41 mentions, 73% positive
Three-stage height adjustment gives control over cut height for different grass types and terrain.
cons: The tool-only purchase means a high entry cost; adding a battery and charger pushes the overall price significantly beyond most cordless grass shears. — 23 mentions, 65% negative
The blade guard is thin plastic and can bend if stored in a crowded shed or left unprotected.
products.verdict: For Makita 18V LXT system owners managing large ornamental beds, the XMU04Z’s wide swath and marathon runtime justify the premium. Gardeners without existing Makita batteries will get more for their money from a lighter 2-in-1 kit.
products.review:
The Makita XMU04Z stands apart with a 6-5/16-inch cutting swath—the widest in this comparison—and a sustained 2,500 SPM that makes quick work of large flower beds and boxwood borders. Three height presets (9/16, 3/4, and 1 inch) handle everything from tight turf edges to taller ornamental grasses. Paired with a 5.0Ah battery, the tool can deliver up to 200 minutes of runtime, enough to complete a full morning’s cleanup without interruption.

This shear is purpose-built for existing Makita 18V LXT system users who need pro-grade efficiency. The tool-only price makes sense if you already have batteries and charger, but for newcomers the total system cost lands well above typical grass shears. The thin plastic blade guard requires careful storage to avoid warping. Occasional trimmers and those who want a convertible shrub shear attachment will find better value elsewhere.

The double-shear blade action cuts cleanly through fescues and dense ground cover without tearing, even after prolonged use. Build quality is solid, and the weight—while heavier than 7.2V alternatives—dampens vibration and keeps the blade tracking straight. Using a lower-capacity 2.0Ah battery reduces runtime noticeably, but for most dedicated garden beds a 5.0Ah pack easily lasts the job.

💡 💡 Tip: Keep the blade guard in place and store the shear flat to prevent bending, and pair it with a 5.0Ah battery for the claimed runtime.

Its battery runtime is extremely short, often providing only a couple of minutes before it powers down, and the plastic build is flimsy, so expect to treat it as a disposable seasonal helper rather than a lasting tool. For those with zero tolerance for weight and only the tiniest touch-up needs, it fills a niche.

💡 💡 Tip: Charge it fully before each use and plan for only a couple of minutes of trimming — this is for spot work, not sustained edging.

buyingGuide.heading: Voltage
buyingGuide.body:
Voltage directly determines cutting torque. 7.2V shears are featherweights that excel at soft grass and deadheading annuals, but their motors stall on dense or woody material. The hidden trade-off: every extra push past the design limit chews through plastic gears that are almost universal in this voltage class.

20V models trade some portability for real muscle. They power through thick ground cover and shrub growth without bogging down. If your yard includes even a single boxwood or ornamental grass clump that a 7.2V unit couldn't handle, stepping up to 18-20V prevents frustration and extends tool life.

buyingGuide.heading: Blade Width & Type
buyingGuide.body:
Grass blade widths range from 3.5 to 6.3 inches. A wider swath speeds up edging along long flower beds, but adds weight and can feel clumsy around tight corners. Dual-action blades—where two blades move in opposite directions—cut cleaner and reduce vibration compared to single-action designs.

If you'll use the shrub attachment, check the blade length. 6-8 inches is adequate for light shaping, but miniaturized hedge blades lack the leverage of full-sized trimmers. Attempting woody branches thicker than 1/4 inch risks jamming the mechanism, no matter the blade size.

buyingGuide.heading: Weight
buyingGuide.body:
This category demands one-handed control for edging and detail work. Shears weighing under 2 lbs can be used for 20 minutes without fatigue; 3-4 lb models become tiresome for anyone with limited grip strength. The physics are simple: every ounce you hold above shoulder height multiplies strain.

The lightest options—around 0.8 to 1.6 lbs—are best for seniors or long trimming sessions. If you have a large yard but need power, consider a 20V shear with a secondary battery and plan breaks, as arm fatigue will set in faster than the battery drains.

buyingGuide.heading: Battery System
buyingGuide.body:
Built-in batteries are convenient for occasional use but cannot be swapped mid-job. When the charge drops, you're done. Swappable battery platforms (WORX PowerShare, Ryobi ONE+, Makita LXT) let you use packs you already own for longer runtime and lower long-term cost.

USB-C charging on budget models is handy for traveling or charging from a power bank, but some units have finicky charge circuitry—non-standard cables can prevent a full charge. For sustained work, a dedicated charger and spare battery remain more reliable.

buyingGuide.heading: Durability of Drive Mechanism
buyingGuide.body:
The drive mechanism—typically plastic gears in sub-$50 shears—is the number-one failure point. Metal gears are rare in this form factor, so the rule is simple: plastic gears = soft, green vegetation only. Even a stray twig can strip teeth instantly.

Higher-priced models often use reinforced composite or dual-action gearing that tolerates light woody growth. Before buying, mentally separate the tool's marketing from its internal parts: if the price feels too low for metal, assume plastic and treat it with care.

faq.question: Why did my cordless grass shear stop working after only a few months?
faq.answer: Most budget shears use plastic gears that can't handle branches thicker than 1/8 inch. Damp grass or woody stems cause rapid wear. Stick to soft grass and thin annuals, or upgrade to a 20V model with metal or reinforced gears if your yard includes shrubs. If the tool already failed, replacement is typically more cost-effective than repair.

faq.question: Can a 7.2-volt grass shear cut through thick ornamental grasses?
faq.answer: 7.2V shears struggle with dense, fibrous ornamental grasses like pampas or miscanthus. The motor bogs down and plastic gears may strip. A 20V shear with wider blades and higher torque, such as the Greenworks 24V, slices through these plants without stalling. For truly tough clumps, use a hedge trimmer instead.

faq.question: How long should a cordless grass shear battery last on one charge for real yard work?
faq.answer: In real conditions, expect 15–30 minutes of continuous cutting from a fully charged battery, not the 'up to 1 hour' claims. Cutting damp grass, thick stems, or running the shear constantly drains it faster. That covers edging a typical suburban yard; larger properties need a spare battery.

faq.question: What is the difference between 7.2V and 20V cordless grass shears?
faq.answer: 7.2V shears are featherlight (under 2 lbs) and perfect for soft grass edging and deadheading annuals. 20V models offer more power to cut through thicker growth and often work with interchangeable battery systems for other tools. The trade-off is weight: 20V shears weigh 3–5 lbs, enough to cause arm fatigue on long trimming sessions. Choose based on your vegetation and how long you trim.
